channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: This Republican voted to convict Trump. Now he's up for reelection. Can he survive?
item.description: Louisiana Sen. Bill Cassidy was one of seven Republican senators who voted to convict President Trump. Now he's running for reelection in a race that will test Trump's hold on the GOP.
item.content:encoded:
Louisiana Sen. Bill Cassidy was one of seven Republican senators who voted to convict President Trump. Now he's running for reelection in a race that will test Trump's hold on the GOP.

(Image credit: Annie Flanagan for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: Elephants eat their crops. Farmers strike back. It's a war that's only getting worse
item.description: In Sri Lanka, the islanders revere elephants. But for farmers, there's rising tension that's leading to more fatal encounters — for both humans and hungry pachyderms.
item.content:encoded:
In Sri Lanka, the islanders revere elephants. But for farmers, there's rising tension that's leading to more fatal encounters — for both humans and hungry pachyderms.

(Image credit: Brent Stirton/Getty Images/Reportage Archive)
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: The townspeople of Vilseck, Germany, worry that Trump may pull out 5,000 U.S. troops
item.description: President Trump's troop withdrawal threat rattles residents of a small Bavarian town reliant on U.S. military personnel and their families for both income and friendships built over decades.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's troop withdrawal threat rattles residents of a small Bavarian town reliant on U.S. military personnel and their families for both income and friendships built over decades.

(Image credit: Lisi Niesner)
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Colleges got more rural students to apply. The challenge is getting them to attend
item.description: Some of the nation's most selective institutions are slowly increasing their rural enrollment with the help of millions of dollars from a rural alumnus of the University of Chicago.
item.content:encoded:
Some of the nation's most selective institutions are slowly increasing their rural enrollment with the help of millions of dollars from a rural alumnus of the University of Chicago.

(Image credit: Lucy Lu)
item.dc:creator: Jon Marcus

item.title: This Republican voted to convict Trump. Now he's up for reelection. Can he survive?
item.description: Louisiana Sen. Bill Cassidy was one of seven Republican senators who voted to convict President Trump. Now he's running for reelection in a race that will test Trump's hold on the GOP.
item.content:encoded:
Louisiana Sen. Bill Cassidy was one of seven Republican senators who voted to convict President Trump. Now he's running for reelection in a race that will test Trump's hold on the GOP.

(Image credit: Annie Flanagan for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: Trump says Islamic State group leader was killed in a joint U.S.-Nigerian mission
item.description: Trump announced the joint operation in Africa's most populous country in a late-night social media post. He said Abu Bakr al-Mainuki was second in command of the Islamic State group globally.
item.content:encoded:
Trump announced the joint operation in Africa's most populous country in a late-night social media post. He said Abu Bakr al-Mainuki was second in command of the Islamic State group globally.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The Eurovision Song Contest reaches its grand final with pop and protests
item.description: The final of the Eurovision Contest arrives Saturday, with tight security and rainy weather failing to dent the enthusiasm of fans, or the opposition of critics who think Israel shouldn't be invited.
item.content:encoded:
The final of the Eurovision Contest arrives Saturday, with tight security and rainy weather failing to dent the enthusiasm of fans, or the opposition of critics who think Israel shouldn't be invited.

(Image credit: Martin Meissner)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Israel says it killed the leader of Hamas' military wing
item.description: Israel says it has killed the leader of Hamas' military wing, one of the architects of the Oct, 7, 2023, attacks that triggered the war in Gaza.
item.content:encoded:
Israel says it has killed the leader of Hamas' military wing, one of the architects of the Oct, 7, 2023, attacks that triggered the war in Gaza.

(Image credit: Jehad Alshrafi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Supreme Court is death knell for Virginia's Democratic-friendly congressional maps
item.description: The new map was drawn by Democrats and approved by Virginia voters, but the state Supreme Court declared the referendum null and void because lawmakers failed to follow the proper procedures to get the issue on the ballot.
item.content:encoded:
The new map was drawn by Democrats and approved by Virginia voters, but the state Supreme Court declared the referendum null and void because lawmakers failed to follow the proper procedures to get the issue on the ballot.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Colorado's Democratic governor will let Trump ally Tina Peters out of prison early
item.description: Gov. Jared Polis' controversial commutation follows a pressure campaign by the Trump administration to free Tina Peters, an ex-county official who was convicted of tampering with election equipment.
item.content:encoded:
Gov. Jared Polis' controversial commutation follows a pressure campaign by the Trump administration to free Tina Peters, an ex-county official who was convicted of tampering with election equipment.

(Image credit: Scott Crabtree)
item.dc:creator: Bente Birkeland
